name: business-bankruptcy-marketing-kit
description: Nevada-compliant marketing kit for business bankruptcy attorneys (Chapter 11, Subchapter V, Chapter 7 business). 7 compliance moats: SCR 192 + RPC 7.1 + 11 USC § 528 [ANCHOR], Subchapter V vs. standard Chapter 11 (SBRA 2019, no disclosure statement, no committee, $7.5M threshold, modified absolute priority) [SHARPEST], § 547 preference action exposure (90-day/1-year lookback, ordinary course defense) [SHARPEST #2], § 365 executory contracts (120-day commercial lease deadline, assumption cure, § 502(b)(6) cap), NRS 78/86 Nevada entity law intersection (revocation, zone of insolvency fiduciary duty, UFTA 4-year lookback), § 1129(b) cram-down and absolute priority rule, § 363 cash collateral and § 364 DIP financing.
anchor_moat: SCR 192 + RPC 7.1 + 11 USC § 528 — missing debt relief agency disclosure is a federal statutory violation in business bankruptcy attorney advertising; courts in the 9th Circuit have applied § 528 broadly beyond consumer debtors.
sharpest_moat: Subchapter V (SBRA 2019, 11 USC §§ 1181–1195): no disclosure statement, no creditors' committee, modified absolute priority rule (§ 1191), debtor-only plan, 90-day deadline, $7.5M eligibility threshold. Every Nevada business under $7.5M in debt qualifies. No AI tool mentions it. Attorney marketing standard-Chapter-11-only loses 80% of small business clients to Subchapter V practitioners.
sharpest_moat_2: § 547 preference actions: payments to creditors within 90 days (1 year for insiders) are trustee clawback targets. AI tools advise 'pay vendors before filing' — creating preference action targets. Ordinary course, new value, and contemporaneous exchange defenses exist but require pre-filing strategy.
nevada_statutes: NRS 78.175 (annual list requirement — corporation good standing)
NRS 78.307 (Nevada piercing corporate veil / alter ego)
NRS 78.747 (LLC alter ego — strong protections with exceptions)
NRS 86.263 (LLC annual list requirement — good standing)
NRS 86.274 (LLC manager fiduciary duty — shifts toward creditors in zone of insolvency)
NRS 104 (Nevada UCC — perfection of security interests continuing in bankruptcy)
NRS 112.180 (Nevada UFTA — fraudulent transfers, 4-year lookback via § 544(b))
NRS 112.210 (Nevada UFTA statute of limitations — 4 years)
NRS 17.150 (Nevada judgment lien on real property)
SCR 192 (attorney advertising disclaimer — This is an advertisement.)
RPC 7.1 (no false/misleading statements — specialization + outcome guarantees)
RPC 7.2 (referral fee prohibition — CPA co-marketing is educational only)
federal_law: 11 USC § 101(31) (insider definition — 1-year preference lookback)
11 USC § 341 (creditors' meeting — applies to all chapters)
11 USC § 361 (adequate protection — cash payments, replacement liens, equity cushion)
11 USC § 362(a) (automatic stay)
11 USC § 362(b) (automatic stay exceptions — tax authority, criminal, domestic support, police power)
11 USC § 362(d) (motion for relief from stay)
11 USC § 363(a) (cash collateral definition)
11 USC § 363(c)(2) (cash collateral use — court order or consent required)
11 USC § 363(k) (credit bid by secured creditor in § 363 sale)
11 USC § 364(a)-(d) (DIP financing — ordinary course, secured, superpriority, priming)
11 USC § 365(a) (executory contract assumption or rejection)
11 USC § 365(b)(1) (assumption requires cure of defaults)
11 USC § 365(c)(1) (anti-assignment clauses survive for certain contracts)
11 USC § 365(d)(4) (commercial lease 120-day assume-or-reject deadline)
11 USC § 365(n) (software licensee election to retain rights on rejection)
11 USC § 502(b)(6) (landlord rejection damage cap — 1 year or 15% of term)
11 USC § 528(a)(4) (debt relief agency disclosure — required in all bankruptcy attorney ads)
11 USC § 544(b) (trustee state law avoidance — extends Nevada UFTA to 4 years)
11 USC § 547 (preference actions — 90-day/1-year lookback, $6,825 business threshold)
11 USC § 547(c) (preference defenses — ordinary course, new value, contemporaneous exchange)
11 USC § 548 (fraudulent transfer — 2-year federal lookback)
11 USC § 553 (bank right of setoff — deposit account vs. pre-petition debt)
11 USC § 1102 (creditors' committee — appointed in standard Chapter 11)
11 USC § 1121(b) (plan exclusivity period — 120 days, extendable)
11 USC § 1129(a)(7) (best interests test — floor for all creditors)
11 USC § 1129(b) (cram-down — fair and equitable, no unfair discrimination)
11 USC § 1129(b)(2)(A) (fair and equitable — secured creditors)
11 USC § 1129(b)(2)(B) (fair and equitable — unsecured, absolute priority rule)
11 USC § 1181 (Subchapter V — scope and definitions)
11 USC § 1182 (Subchapter V — eligibility — $7.5M non-contingent debt limit)
11 USC § 1183 (Subchapter V — standing trustee)
11 USC § 1189(b) (Subchapter V — 90-day plan filing deadline)
11 USC § 1191 (Subchapter V — plan confirmation, modified absolute priority rule)
26 USC § 6672 (trust fund recovery penalty — follows responsible officer personally)
FTC 2023 Testimonial Rules (review request compliance)
SBRA 2019 — Pub. L. 116-54 (Small Business Reorganization Act — created Subchapter V)
case_law: Till v. SCS Credit Corp., 541 U.S. 465 (2004) — formula rate for cram-down interest on secured debt
Czyzewski v. Jevic Holding Corp., 580 U.S. 451 (2017) — structured dismissal cannot distribute assets in violation of priority

coverage: Skill #252: Personal Bankruptcy (Chapter 7 / Chapter 13 — NRS 21.090 homestead $605K, means test, § 523 discharge, § 362 stay, NRS 116.3116 HOA super-priority, § 528 disclosure) — COMPLETE

Skill #253: Business Bankruptcy (Chapter 11 / Subchapter V / Chapter 7 business — Sub V eligibility, preference actions, § 365 leases, absolute priority, § 363 cash collateral, NRS 78/86 entity) — COMPLETE
